title: Bridge Essay: The myth of the Bayesian brain.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind.

**The myth of the Bayesian brain.**. European journal of applied physiology.. PMID: 40569419. DOI: 10.1007/s00421-025-05855-6. The Bayesian brain hypothesis-the idea that neural systems implement or approximate Bayesian inference-has become adominant framework in cognitive neuroscience over the past two decades. While mathematically elegant and conceptually unifying, this paper argues that the hypothesis occupies an ambiguous territory between useful metaphor and testable, biologically plausible mechanistic explanation.
